title: Public Data
tagline: Open datasets generated and published by the Podcast Index project.
intro: Podcast Index produces a number of public data exports — full feed catalogs, recommendation lists, value-block dumps, activity stats, and infrastructure pointers — all served as static files under public.podcastindex.org and a few sibling subdomains. They are free to mirror and use; please don't hammer them in tight loops, and prefer the SQLite/CSV catalog dumps over live API scraping when you need bulk data.

sections.name: Catalog snapshots
sections.description: Full feed catalogs. The SQLite dumps are the canonical bulk artifacts — load them directly with sqlite3 or any client.

datasets.name: All live feeds — SQLite
datasets.description: Every feed currently being polled, with title, URL, owner, iTunes ID, host, language, categories, generator, and lastest-episode timestamps. The most heavily used artifact downstream — most third-party mirrors of the Podcast Index data start here.

datasets.name: Dead feeds — CSV (id only)
datasets.description: Lightweight CSV of dead feed IDs and the ID of the feed they duplicate (if any). Use this if you only need to reconcile your own list of feeds against the dead set.

datasets.name: Problematic feeds — CSV
datasets.description: Dead feeds that have an entry in the problematic-feeds table. Includes feed URL, generator, author, owner, iTunes ID, the reason code, and the editor's note. Hosting-company brand names are stripped from the note column for cleaner pattern analysis.

sections.name: Discovery & recommendations
sections.description: Curated and computed lists derived from podcast 2.0 tags and feed metadata.

datasets.name: Recommendations — all media
datasets.description: Show recommendations mined from <podcast:podroll> tags across the whole index, ranked by mention count.

datasets.name: Recommendations — video only
datasets.description: The same recommendation graph, filtered to feeds whose <podcast:medium> or content profile marks them as video.

datasets.name: Recommendations — music only
datasets.description: The same recommendation graph, filtered to feeds whose <podcast:medium> or content profile marks them as music.

datasets.name: Value-enabled feeds — CSV
datasets.description: All feeds carrying a <podcast:value> block, grouped by host. Useful for surveying Lightning/Hive value-for-value adoption across the index.

datasets.name: Hosting provider stats
datasets.description: Top hosting providers by live feed count. A quick read on the shape of the podcasting hosting market.

sections.name: Activity & stats
sections.description: Time-series counts and rolling new-feed reports.

datasets.name: 24-hour feed report — HTML
datasets.description: Human-readable daily report: counts of new feeds added in the last 24 hours, top generators, hour-by-hour add rate, and keyword-based scoring of the new-arrival mix.

datasets.name: Newly-added feeds (last 24h) — CSV
datasets.description: Sliding-window CSV of every feed added to the index in the past 24 hours. Datestamped daily snapshots are also published as newlyAddedFeeds24hoursYYYY-MM-DD.csv.

datasets.name: Hourly counts
datasets.description: Hour-bucketed aggregate counts: feeds added, episodes added, and API requests served. Useful for charting recent index activity.

datasets.name: Daily counts
datasets.description: Day-bucketed aggregate counts with a longer retention window than the hourly file — better for long-term trend lines.

sections.name: Infrastructure feeds
sections.description: Operational pointers used by clients of the Podcast Index ecosystem.

datasets.name: Healthy Hive RPC nodes
datasets.description: Plain-text list of Hive blockchain RPC nodes that have recently passed a health check. Consumed by Podping listeners that need a working node to subscribe to.

datasets.name: Live tracking firehose — current window
datasets.description: Most recent ~200-second window of newly-added or updated feeds and items, as JSON. Polled by partner mobile apps to keep their local catalogs current. Per-window archives are also written under /<unix-timestamp>.
